answer:
This rebuttal of claims of Caucasians' natural superiority appears in Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation, Vol. III*, Chapter 48 ("De la supériorité des peuples d’espèce caucasienne sur les peuples des autres espèces. — Des causes").[^1]

The Athens line reads: "In the republic of Athens, there were, it is assured, twenty thousand citizens and four hundred thousand slaves: that was twenty slaves for one free man, approximately the same proportion observed in the colonies between whites and blacks."[^2]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation, Vol. III*, [Chapter 48](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/48-de-la-superiorite-des-peuples-d-espece-caucasienne/translation/).

[^2]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation, Vol. III*, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/48-de-la-superiorite-des-peuples-d-espece-caucasienne/translation/#p-b496c9).

args.query: twenty thousand citizens and four hundred thousand slaves
